publishedAbstract: There may be instances where Cluster Admins will want to grant the ability for users to create Object Buckets/Object Bucket Claims in their respective namespace using the Admin role, but not having the Cluster Admin role. This solution will cover this process. **Prerequisite:** Follow the respective OCP documentation to create users and assign the respective role (admin) ot the user in the desired namespace. [How to create new users in OpenShift with htpasswd and OAuth](https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/openshift-htpasswd-oauth) [Chapter 9. Using RBAC to define and apply permissions](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/openshift_container_platform/4.19/html/authentication_and_authorization/using-rbac) **NOTE:** The user will need admin access to both the `openshift-storage` namespace as well as the namespace where the desired bucket creation will occur. This is because the NooBaa API calls go through the `noobaa-operator` that is in the `openshift-storage` namespace. Additionally, if using the NooBaa CLI, those commands must be executed in the `openshift-storage` namespace. **Optional (If Wanting to Use the NooBaa CLI):** [2.2. Accessing the Multicloud Object Gateway from the MCG command-line interface](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_openshift_data_foundation/4.19/html/managing_hybrid_and_multicloud_resources/accessing-the-multicloud-object-gateway-with-your-applications_rhodf#accessing-the-Multicloud-object-gateway-from-the-mcg-command-line-interface_rhodf)
publishedTitle: Allowing Users to Create NooBaa Object Bucket Claims without Having the "cluster-admin" Role - Multicloud Object Gateway (MCG)
